checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=147707962.35 (2011-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=133543041.39 (2010-12-31); SalesRevenueNet=514787061.00 (2011 annual) vs SalesRevenueNet=455088925.36 (2010 annual)

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=140747112.54 (2013-12-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=135447065.23 (2012-12-31); SalesRevenueServicesNet=540259959.61 (2013 annual) vs SalesRevenueServicesNet=531876083.41 (2012 annual)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=129856604.37 (2011-12-31) grew to Goodwill=143308197.05 (2012-12-31), a 10.4% increase, while SalesRevenueNet=514787061.00 (2011 annual) grew to SalesRevenueServicesNet=531876083.41 (2012 annual), only a 3.3% increase
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=1040043.53 (2011-06-30) jumped to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=15145066.70 (2011-12-31), a large non-linear swing within the same fiscal year

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Gross margin implied by SalesRevenueNet=514787061.00 (2011) and CostOfServices=187990137.00 (2011) = 63.5%, declining to SalesRevenueServicesNet=540259959.61 (2013) and CostOfServices=212475629.54 (2013) = 60.7%
No inventory line item is present in the provided data set (service company), limiting inventory-based analysis

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss for 2013: 3936918.70 (Q1), 11226298.84 (Q2), 4554047.50 (Q3), 3922398.03 (Q4) - showing substantial quarter-to-quarter volatility rather than smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=6614168.63 (2010-12-31) fell to AccountsPayableCurrent=2219848.58 (2011-12-31), a 66% decline, while SalesRevenueNet grew from 455088925.36 (2010) to 514787061.00 (2011)
AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=66223365.12 (2010-12-31) declined to AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=54730248.82 (2011-12-31), a 17% decline, despite revenue growth of 13% in the same period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2012-03-12, only three days after the original 10-K filed 2012-03-09 (accession 0001193125-12-106588)
NT 10-K filed 2007-03-16, followed by NT 10-Q filings on 2007-05-10, 2007-08-09, and 2007-11-09, indicating a period of chronic late filings

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Point-in-time data spans annual and quarterly figures from 2009 through 2013 for Assets, Liabilities, Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, and cash flow statement items across multiple 10-K and 10-Q filings
No inventory, allowance for doubtful accounts, or segment-level data are provided, limiting some checklist items (e.g., CL4 inventory analysis)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential understatement/deferral of trade payables and accrued expense liabilities in FY2011 relative to business growth, which would understate expenses and overstate net income in the period; reversal or catch-up in later periods (e.g., FY2013 accrued liabilities jumping back up to 60.75M) could then explain part of the subsequent earnings decline.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsPayableCurrent=6614168.63 (2010-12-31) fell to AccountsPayableCurrent=2219848.58 (2011-12-31) while SalesRevenueNet grew from 455088925.36 (2010) to 514787061.00 (2011)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible capitalization of costs into goodwill/other noncurrent assets rather than expensing them, inflating asset base and understating operating expenses in periods when goodwill grew faster than revenue (FY2012) and when OtherAssetsNoncurrent showed abrupt non-linear jumps within a fiscal year.

rationale_evidence.quote: Goodwill=129856604.37 (2011-12-31) to Goodwill=143308197.05 (2012-12-31), +10.4%, versus revenue growth of only 3.3% (SalesRevenueNet=514787061.00 (2011) to SalesRevenueServicesNet=531876083.41 (2012))

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible timing shifts in SG&A expense recognition across periods (expenses deferred into later periods) given SG&A grew materially faster than revenue in FY2013 (6.2% vs 1.6%), contributing to a sharp margin compression and ~50% net income decline that could partly reflect catch-up recognition of previously deferred costs rather than a purely organic deterioration.

rationale_evidence.quote: SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpense=284839426.18 (2012 annual) to SellingGeneralAndAdministrativeExpense=302532871.79 (2013 annual), a 6.2% increase, versus SalesRevenueServicesNet growth of only 1.6% (531876083.41 (2012) to 540259959.61 (2013))
NetIncomeLoss=47729467.15 (2012 annual) fell to NetIncomeLoss=23639663.07 (2013 annual), approximately a 50% decline

top_signals: Sharp decline in AccountsPayableCurrent and AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent in FY2011 despite double-digit revenue growth, suggesting possible liability understatement
Goodwill growth outpacing revenue growth in FY2012, and erratic OtherAssetsNoncurrent balances suggesting possible capitalization issues
Gross margin decline from ~63.5% (2011) to ~60.7% (2013) combined with SG&A growing faster than revenue in 2013, driving a ~50% net income decline

Quick 10-K/A filed only 3 days after the original 10-K (2012-03-09 to 2012-03-12) and a long history of SEC UPLOAD/CORRESP correspondence across multiple years, indicating recurring regulatory scrutiny

Net income and operating cash flow both declined in tandem (no major CFO/NI divergence), and earnings show natural quarterly volatility rather than suspicious smoothness, which partially offsets fraud concern
